digest.component_or_area: MCP optional auth documentation and ADR
propositions: Write an operator guide docs/mcp-auth.md covering enabling HTTP transport plus auth, all five verifier modes with flat-config examples, the auth_strategies vocabulary, and the one-instance-per-strategy-type limitation.

Document the security model: resource-server-only (no AS/DCR), the composite as the single fail-closed audience choke point per RFC 8707, no token passthrough, DNS-rebinding/Origin defaults, and non-loopback bind requirements.

Document behind-a-proxy deployment where TLS terminates at the edge and rebar binds loopback, requiring external host/origin allowlists and auth_resource_server_url set to the external https URL driving discovery.

Document the three interactive-client connection paths (static bearer header, automatic OAuth via DCR/CIMD, pre-registered client-id/secret) and residual risks (deferred per-tool authz, edge-delegated rate-limiting, mandated static-token entropy).
Author an ADR recording the resource-server-only and build-own-verifiers decisions, the audience choke point, the five verifier modes, mitigated vulnerability classes, and explicitly deferred items.
Perform final consistency verification that docs/mcp-reference.md and docs/env-vars.md are regenerated for the integrated key set and that the manifest and drift gates pass on the combined branch.
